title: Self-Healing Disaster Recovery System
summary: Implemented automated DR runbooks with self-healing triggers on GCP for production critical services.

content.value: Quotient's production services had documented DR runbooks but they relied on on-call engineers to manually execute multi-step recovery procedures. During incidents, human error and stress led to prolonged outages, violating SLA commitments.

Converted all DR runbooks into Ansible playbooks parameterised by failure scenario. A Python orchestrator subscribed to PagerDuty webhooks and, upon alert firing, matched alert labels to the appropriate playbook and triggered automated execution.

Built a confidence scoring system: low-risk remediations (pod restart, cache flush) executed automatically; medium-risk (traffic failover) required a single Slack approval; high-risk (data restore) paged a human. GCP Cloud Monitoring dashboards provided real-time visibility during automated recovery.
MTTR reduced by 90% — from an average of 45 minutes to under 5 minutes for automated scenarios. Achieved 99.95% SLA uptime across all critical services for two consecutive quarters.
